skill.name: senior-qa
skill.description: QA and testing skill with guidance for test strategy, automation patterns, coverage review, and E2E scaffolding. It includes reference documentation and simple Python CLI starter scripts for QA workflows.
skill.summary: Review QA practices and use starter scripts for coverage and E2E testing workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static command execution, weak cryptography, environment access, and reconnaissance detections are false positives from markdown fences, prose, and examples. The only confirmed issue is low-risk filesystem output: the Python scripts can write JSON results to a user-supplied path. No prompt injection, network access, subprocess execution, credential access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled Report Output Path
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The scripts write JSON results to the path supplied with --output. This is a normal CLI feature, but it can overwrite files if a user supplies a sensitive path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write calls are direct and visible at the cited lines. Risk is limited because writes require an explicit --output argument and no privilege escalation is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Report Output Path
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The scripts write JSON results to the path supplied with --output. This is a normal CLI feature, but it can overwrite files if a user supplies a sensitive path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write calls are direct and visible at the cited lines. Risk is limited because writes require an explicit --output argument and no privilege escalation is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Markdown Command Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static detections marked markdown fences, inline file names, and user-facing shell examples as Ruby backtick execution. No code path executes these strings.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation lines or markdown code fences, not executable Ruby or Python code. No subprocess, os.system, or shell execution API was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static detections occurred in prose and argparse descriptions, not cryptographic code. No MD5, SHA-1, cipher, hashing, or password storage implementation was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited files found documentation text and simple CLI descriptions only. There is no cryptographic API usage at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines are documentation headings about anti-patterns and examples. They do not collect host, process, network, or operating system information.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The locations are markdown headings or bullets with no executable logic. The scripts do not call host reconnaissance commands or APIs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Environment File Access Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged line is a setup example that copies .env.example to .env. It does not read, print, or transmit environment secrets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a markdown shell example for local configuration. No script reads .env files or environment variables.

content.user_title: Review QA Strategy and Testing Starter Scripts
content.value_statement: Testing programs need repeatable structure before automation is reliable. This skill provides QA guidance, command examples, and starter scripts for coverage reports and E2E scaffolding.
actual_capabilities: Documents QA strategy, automation patterns, and best practices in three reference guides.
Provides Python CLI starter scripts for test suite, coverage, and E2E workflows.
Validates that a target path exists before producing a report.
Prints a simple status report with target path and finding count.
Optionally writes JSON-formatted script results to a user-specified output path.
limitations: Scripts are starter templates and do not inspect source code deeply.
The current scripts do not generate concrete test files.
Does not run test frameworks, browsers, or CI workflows.
Users must review and adapt references for their stack.

use_cases.title: Plan a testing baseline
use_cases.description: Use the references to define unit, integration, and E2E testing expectations for a project.

use_cases.title: Start coverage review
use_cases.description: Run the coverage starter script to produce a simple report shell for a React or Next.js codebase.

use_cases.title: Standardize QA language
use_cases.description: Share common QA practices and anti-patterns across teams before adding deeper automation.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize QA references
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to summarize the QA practices that apply to my React project at [project path].

prompt_templates.title: Create a test plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing strategy references to draft a test plan for [feature name] in my Next.js application.

prompt_templates.title: Run starter analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the coverage analyzer starter script against [project path] and explain the report fields.

prompt_templates.title: Design an E2E rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the E2E scaffolding guidance to design a phased browser testing rollout for [application context].

output_examples.input: Summarize the testing approach for a new checkout feature.
output: Recommended layers: unit tests for pricing rules, integration tests for API flow, and E2E tests for checkout completion.
Primary risks: payment failures, validation gaps, and inconsistent browser behavior.
Next step: map each acceptance criterion to at least one test type.

output_examples.input: Run the coverage starter script on my project and explain the result.
output: The script validated the target path and produced a simple success report.
The current template does not calculate real coverage metrics.
Use the report structure as a starting point for a deeper analyzer.

output_examples.input: Help me introduce E2E testing for a Next.js application.
output: Start with critical login, navigation, and submission paths.
Keep browser tests focused on user-visible behavior.
Run the smallest reliable suite in CI before expanding coverage.

best_practices: Review the starter scripts before running them in a repository.
Use temporary output paths when testing the JSON report option.
Turn the reference guidance into project-specific test acceptance criteria.
anti_patterns: Treating the starter scripts as complete test automation.
Writing tests only to raise coverage numbers.
Running output commands against sensitive file paths.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete test suites?
faq.answer: No. The current scripts are starter templates and need project-specific implementation.

faq.question: Can it calculate real code coverage?
faq.answer: No. The coverage script currently reports template results, not measured coverage data.

faq.question: Does it modify my source files?
faq.answer: No source changes are made by default. The scripts only write a report when --output is provided.

faq.question: Which projects is it aimed at?
faq.answer: The documentation targets React, Next.js, Node.js, and related web application testing workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe to run?
faq.answer: It is low risk when reviewed first and run with a non-sensitive output path.

faq.question: How should teams use it?
faq.answer: Use it as QA planning material and as a scaffold for custom automation work.
